what_it_does: Declares the layout face of a design-system DNA: reusable spatial archetypes, typed content slots, per-frame zones and focal slots, reflow policies, dial bindings, and data-to-slot maps for rendering slides, graphs, app views, and decision surfaces.
observations: 19 slot_types are declared, from heading and figure to container plate and minted types check-list and options.

14 archetypes mix static presentation forms (trio, duo, split-bleed, field, grid, stack, immersive, plate-internal) with data-bound app views (session-card, channel-view, board-view, transcript-viz) and the interactive decision-card.
Each archetype declares a frame, zones in reading order, slots with accepted types, optional flags, surface values, exactly one focal slot, a reflow policy, applicable dials, and evidence tags.
The meta section explicitly rejects numeric priority; focality is frame-scoped and resolves nearest-scope-wins when frames nest.
Dynamic archetypes use slot_map to bind record fields to slot ids, and some declare render_kind (slide, graph, zones).
decision-card includes take semantics: the options slot writes decision_take and supports both portrait and landscape reflow.
plate-internal is recursive: a plate is itself a frame, enabling composition at every scale.
corpus_additions records diagonal seams, a proposed divider archetype, and multi-scale evidence, but does not add the divider to the archetypes object.
Evidence strings mix deck slide references, variant IDs, and screenshot names, tagged as deck-first or mockup-derived.
standouts: decision-card archetype with operator-choice semantics and take/write-back.
plate-internal recursion making every plate a composable frame.
Frame-scoped focal-slot rule and explicit rejection of global priority.
slot_map data binding bridging record fields to layout slots.
Minted options slot type for decidable choices with recommended flags.
conventions: Top-level structure: meta, slot_types, archetypes, corpus_additions.
Archetypes share keys: title, about, frame, zones, focal, reflow, dials, evidence.
Zones list slots in reading order; each slot has id, accepts array, optional boolean, and surface enum.
Proportions are referenced from grammar.json and never redeclared in this file.
Evidence is tagged with deck vs mockup/variant provenance.
Container slot type plate references the internal archetype plate-internal.
concerns: Several archetypes reference frames (title-main-footer, split-hero-text) that are not defined in this file.
field archetype is admitted as mockup-only/system invention, creating slight tension with the deck-first provenance claim.
stack mixes a bands share array with a zones structure; the relationship between share values and slot allocation is not specified here.
decision-card includes an id field while other archetypes do not, making the schema inconsistent.
corpus_additions mentions a divider archetype but it is absent from the archetypes object.
Some slot_map target names (cwd, legibility.name, resolve:explanation_source) use conventions not explained locally.
notes: A design-DNA layout registry that treats archetypes as composable frames with typed slots, per-frame focality, and provenance tags, extending into interactive decision surfaces.
questions: Where are frame definitions like title-main-footer and split-hero-text specified?
How are warmth/density dials translated into concrete visual values?
What renderer consumes render_kind and slot_map to produce output?
How is the decision_take write-back persisted and observed?
How are nested plates resolved when both parent and child declare focal slots?
What is the complete list of surface values and their rendering rules?
How do the bands share values in stack interact with the zone-based slot allocation?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it defines spatial archetypes and slot grammar as stated, and additionally carries dynamic view archetypes and interactive decision semantics, which extends but does not contradict the stated layout-DNA purpose.
apparent_themes: Separation of layout structure from warmth/density dials.
Operator decision surfaces as first-class layouts.
intention_signals: To build a generative, reusable layout language where content kinds plug into typed slots, frames compose recursively, and the same DNA can render both static presentations and interactive app/decision interfaces with traceable provenance.
novelty: The combination of static slide archetypes, recursive plate frames, per-frame focal-slot semantics, data-bound app-view archetypes, and an interactive decision-card archetype with take/write-back semantics in a single layout registry.
contribution: A typed, composable layout vocabulary—archetypes, slot types, focal/reflow/dial policies, and field-to-slot maps—that a fused system could reuse to generate, validate, or render layouts across presentation and interactive surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON design-DNA registry of layout archetypes and typed content slots, with per-frame zones, focal slots, reflow policies, dial bindings, evidence tags, and data-to-slot maps for slides, graphs, app views, and decision cards.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a reusable, evidence-backed spatial grammar that binds content kinds to composable frames and supports both static rendering and operator decision interfaces.
suggested_fields: frame_definition to hold the geometry of referenced frames.
render_kind_implementation to map render_kind to renderer or host function.
variant_of to link variant evidence entries to their base archetype.
proposed_edge_kinds: take_writes_to for decision-card options to decision_take.
